section: Chapter 5: The Fundamental Unit of Life

questions.question: What is a cell?
questions.correct_answer: The basic structural and functional unit of life
questions.explanation: Cells are the smallest units capable of performing all life processes.

questions.question: Who discovered the cell?
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke discovered cells in 1665 while observing cork under a microscope.

questions.question: What is the plasma membrane?
questions.correct_answer: The outer boundary of the cell that controls movement of substances
questions.explanation: It is selectively permeable and separates the cell from its environment.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
questions.correct_answer: Movement of substances from higher concentration to lower concentration
questions.explanation: Diffusion helps in exchange of gases such as oxygen and carbon dioxide in cells.

questions.question: What is osmosis?
questions.correct_answer: Movement of water through a selectively permeable membrane
questions.explanation: Water moves from a region of higher water concentration to lower water concentration.

questions.question: What is the function of the cell wall?
questions.correct_answer: Provides support and protection to plant cells
questions.explanation: The cell wall is rigid and mainly composed of cellulose.

questions.question: What is the nucleus?
questions.correct_answer: The control center of the cell containing genetic material
questions.explanation: It contains DNA and chromosomes which control cell activities.

questions.question: What is cytoplasm?
questions.correct_answer: The jelly-like substance inside the cell membrane
questions.explanation: It contains cell organelles and is the site of many metabolic reactions.

questions.question: Which organelle is called the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce energy in the form of ATP.

questions.question: Which organelle performs photosynthesis in plant cells?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll which helps plants perform photosynthesis.

notes.title: Cell
notes.content: Cells are the smallest structural and functional units of life that perform all essential life processes.

notes.title: Plasma Membrane
notes.content: The plasma membrane surrounds the cell and regulates movement of substances in and out of the cell.

notes.title: Nucleus
notes.content: The nucleus contains chromosomes made of DNA which carry genetic information.

notes.title: Cell Organelles
notes.content: Organelles such as mitochondria, ER, Golgi apparatus and lysosomes perform specific functions inside the cell.

notes.title: Cell Division
notes.content: Cell division produces new cells for growth, repair and reproduction. The main types are mitosis and meiosis.

facts.fact: Cells were first observed by Robert Hooke in cork using a microscope in 1665.
Plant cells have a cell wall while animal cells do not.
Mitochondria and plastids contain their own DNA.
Lysosomes are called the suicide bags of the cell because they contain digestive enzymes.
